channel.title: Posts on InfoSec Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on InfoSec Blog

item.title: Alfresco Reset Password Add-on - 0day Vulnerabilities
item.description: This post is as much about the penetration testing process and the 0-day approach as it is about the vulnerability. I discovered a 0-day vulnerability in one of the most used plugin for Password Reset on Alfresco Content Services framework.

item.title: OAuth 2.0 - What could go wrong
item.description: Nowadays modern web applications may implement or use OAuth 2.0 for there own services. In this post we will understand how OAuth 2.0 works and how it can present vulnerabilities.

item.title: Windows Kernel Shellcode : TokenStealer
item.description: A typical Reverse/Bind shellcode will not work when it comes to Windows Kernel Exploitation, most of the time people often use (Nulling out ACLs, Enabling privileges or Replacing process token).

item.title: From read@GLIBC to RCE in X86_64
item.description: While playing some pwn in HackZone CTF, i figured out a new technique for Arbitrary Code Execution only by using the read function from libc.

item.title: CVE-2019-18276 - Suidbash
item.description: This exploit was initially based on an older vulnerability back in 1999 (suidperl). Now after 20 years in 2019 we found the same vulnerability in bash (CVE-2019-18276) was discovered by Ian Pudney.

item.title: Memcached Enumeration
item.description: A server cache is an information technology for the temporary storage of data, to reduce server lag. I find a lot of those technologies in my daily work while doing penetration testing. Memcached is one of them and I’d like to talk about it and how to extract informations from it.

item.title: Sigreturn-Oriented Programming (SROP)
item.description: Sigreturn-oriented programming (SROP) is a exploit development technique used to execute code, this attack employs the same basic assumptions behind the return-oriented programming (ROP) technique.

item.title: CVE-2007-2447 - Samba usermap script
item.description: Samba 3.0.0 - 3.0.25rc3 are subject for Remote Command Injection Vulnerability (CVE-2007-2447), allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands by specifying a username containing shell meta characters.

item.title: Deep dive into DNS messages
item.description: In this Post, I’d like to talk about the packet structure of DNS as well as how to implement it into python script to start asking and receiving answers about domain resolution.

item.title: HC 06 Bluetooth in Ubuntu
item.description: In this article we are going see how to connect HC-06 Bluetooth in Ubuntu, which is commonly used in embedded systems such as arduino. But this time we will see how to use it with linux in order to interact with bluetooth channels.
